page: Amazon Web Services r8ib.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by Intel x86_64 processors. It features 32 virtual CPUs mapped to 16 physical cores, with 2 threads per core. The dedicated CPU allocation operates at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per virtual core.

This instance utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor to manage virtualization overhead. It does not include any local storage, meaning all storage must be provisioned externally. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 33 Gbps. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this instance, and it does not feature any GPU hardware accelerators.

The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of the r8ib.8xlarge makes it a resource-dense option for workloads requiring significant memory capacity relative to compute resources. The lack of local storage and GPU accelerators represents a tradeoff, focusing the hardware profile on memory-bound workloads. This configuration is designed for memory-intensive applications, in-memory databases, large-scale caching layers, and enterprise applications that do not require local NVMe storage or hardware acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services r8ib.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel x86_64 processor running at 3.9 GHz. It provides 256.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per virtual core. Virtualization is managed via the Nitro hypervisor. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on external storage options. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 33 Gbps. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads, such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity without the need for local storage or specialized graphics hardware.
